children.value: I'm Ridley Winters. This is my website where I share some my software engineering hobby projects and tools. I'm a former product manager and now part-time software engineer. For my hobby projects, I am focused on learning Deno and Rust - and a small side obsession with Vulkan and WebGL.
I'm still learning so please take everything you find on this site with a grain of salt.

children.text: End-to-end Development of a Distributed System

children.value: Note: this was originally going to be an article on the Producer-Consumer pattern, but it went a different direction. I need to clean up the naming a bit!

, I've been looking into leaning how to do multithreaded programming in Rust. The scenario I have in mind is to split the render buffer into tiles and let separate threads render each time, where the number of worker threads is a variable.
This requires some mechanism where a pool of threads is created, the tiles are dispatched to the threads awaiting work (i.e. some simple
), the tiles then rendered to a memory buffer, that then is passed back to the main thread to be copied into the main buffer.

Create a pool of a small number worker threads

Create a buffer for a 4k x 4k image
Iterate over  the image as a set of 128x128 tiles
    Assign the tile to over of the worker threads
    Have the thread fill in the tile's pixels

Stitch together the tiles as they are completed
Write the final buffer to disk
Let's start with a single-threaded version to ensure the code is working as expected.
structure. It needs the rectangular region of the overall buffer that is covers: from
since this might be helpful in future threaded version. Lastly, let's give it a

s up to rendered in separate threads, so we use an
to defer allocating the vector until the rendering takes place.

I started with Deno to avoid Babel configuration, but how to build client React code was not clear to me
Webpack complex-feeling configuration turned me to try parcel
Parcel does not seem to do hot loading or impl

npm i --save-dev webpack webpack-cli webpack-dev-server
npm i --save-dev babel-loader
so global installs are not needed
Want to hide this in a single
Make doesn't seem to directly support running multiple processes in the background as part of the command
Encapsulate the bash script to run multiple watchers in that long-string

#
# Use a bash script since we want a single make command to run multiple
# background processes.
#
define DEV_SCRIPT
shutdown() {
    echo kill $(jobs -l |awk '$2 == "|" {print $1; next} {print $2}')
    kill $(jobs -l |awk '$2 == "|" {print $1; next} {print $2}')
    exit 0
}
trap "shutdown" SIGINT SIGTERM

npx nodemon --watch data --ext js,jsx,mdx --exec make build-database &

npx webpack-dev-server \
		--mode development \
		--entry ./src/client.jsx \
		--output-filename client.bundle.js &
wait
endef
export DEV_SCRIPT

dev:
	bash -c "$$DEV_SCRIPT"
